title: MachineLibrary Object
description: The MachineLibrary provides access to machines. Using this object you can import machines and get existing machines using either URL or query to find specific Machines.

methods.name: childAssetURLs
methods.description: Get all assets under given URL.

methods.name: childFolderURLs
methods.description: Get all library folders under given URL.

methods.name: childMachines
methods.description: Get all machines by the given parent folder URL. Returns null, if the URL does not exist.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createFolder
methods.description: Create a new folder in the library. Create the folder under given parent URL with given folder name. Add counting suffix, in case a folder with given name already exists. Throw an error if given URL does not point to a valid folder or the URL is read-only. Also throws an error if given folder name is empty.

methods.name: createQuery
methods.description: Creates a new MachineQuery that is used to query the library for machines matching the query.

methods.name: deleteAsset
methods.description: Delete asset by URL from the library. Throw an error if given URL does not point to a valid asset or the URL is read-only.

methods.name: deleteFolder
methods.description: Delete folder by URL from the library. Any content of the folder will also be deleted. Throw an error if given URL does not point to a valid folder or the URL is read-only.

methods.name: displayName
methods.description: Get the localized display name for a given URL. The URL must point to a folder.

methods.name: doesPathExist
methods.description: Checks if the given URL points to an existing folder or asset in the library.

methods.name: importMachine
methods.description: Import a given machine at a specific location. The machine will be stored in the library. Throws an error, if the given URL is read-only.

methods.name: machineAtURL
methods.description: Get a specific machine by the given URL. Returns null, if the URL does not exist.

methods.name: updateMachine
methods.description: Update a machine in the library. The library overrides the URL by given machine. Throws an error if the URL does not already point to an existing machine.

methods.name: urlByLocation
methods.description: Get the URL for a given LibraryLocations.

properties.name: assetTypeName
properties.description: Get the name of the asset type which can be accessed by the library.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing FFF API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive FFF manufacturing setup and generate a toolpath.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive setup using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select an FFF 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically oriented and arranged within the build area of the selected FFF machine. This script will also create support structures, if required, based on the orientation of each component. Finally, the script generates the toolpath for the active setup and lets the user choose if they wish to post process the resulting toolpath or if they want to simulate it.

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing MJF API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive MJF manufacturing setup and arrange components within the build volume of a 3D printer.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive Arrange using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select an MJF 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically arranged within the build volume of the selected MJF machine.

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing SLA API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive SLA manufacturing setup.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive setup using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select a SLA 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically oriented and arranged within the build area of the selected SLA machine. This script will also create support structures, based on the orientation of each component.

The support and orientation operations are created from a template. The script further demonstrates how to wrap script code into a command such that only one undo entry is created for the entire script instead of one entry per internal action.

assetTypeName.title: MachineLibrary.assetTypeName Property
assetTypeName.description: Get the name of the asset type which can be accessed by the library.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machineLibrary_var.assetTypeName

isValid.title: MachineLibrary.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machineLibrary_var.isValid

objectType.title: MachineLibrary.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machineLibrary_var.objectType

childAssetURLs.title: MachineLibrary.childAssetURLs Method
childAssetURLs.description: Get all assets under given URL.
childAssetURLs.return_description: Returns list of asset URLs at given location.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to the parent folder.

childFolderURLs.title: MachineLibrary.childFolderURLs Method
childFolderURLs.description: Get all library folders under given URL.
childFolderURLs.return_description: Returns list of folder URLs at given location.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to the parent folder.

childMachines.title: MachineLibrary.childMachines Method
childMachines.description: Get all machines by the given parent folder URL. Returns null, if the URL does not exist.
childMachines.return_description: Returns the machine for a valid URL, returns null otherwise.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL of the folder to get machines from.

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing FFF API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive FFF manufacturing setup and generate a toolpath.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive setup using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select an FFF 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically oriented and arranged within the build area of the selected FFF machine. This script will also create support structures, if required, based on the orientation of each component. Finally, the script generates the toolpath for the active setup and lets the user choose if they wish to post process the resulting toolpath or if they want to simulate it.

related_samples.description:

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive Arrange using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select an MJF 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically arranged within the build volume of the selected MJF machine.

related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive SLA manufacturing setup.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive setup using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select a SLA 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. classType.title: MachineLibrary.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createFolder.title: MachineLibrary.createFolder Method
createFolder.description: Create a new folder in the library. Create the folder under given parent URL with given folder name. Add counting suffix, in case a folder with given name already exists. Throw an error if given URL does not point to a valid folder or the URL is read-only. Also throws an error if given folder name is empty.
createFolder.return_description: Returns the URL to the newly created folder

parameters.name: parentUrl
parameters.description: The parent URL for the folder to be created.

parameters.name: folderName
parameters.description: Name of the new folder to be created. Must not be empty.

createQuery.title: MachineLibrary.createQuery Method
createQuery.description: Creates a new MachineQuery that is used to query the library for machines matching the query.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = machineLibrary_var.createQuery(location)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = machineLibrary_var.createQuery(location, vendor, model)

createQuery.return_description: Returns a new MachineQuery. The query is predefined by given parameter.

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: The location specifies the LibraryLocations where to search for in the machine library.

parameters.name: vendor
parameters.description: The vendor specifies the vendor of the machine. The default empty vendor applies to all machines.   This is an optional argument whose default value is "".

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: The model specifies the model of the machine. The default empty model applies to all machines.   This is an optional argument whose default value is "".

deleteAsset.title: MachineLibrary.deleteAsset Method
deleteAsset.description: Delete asset by URL from the library. Throw an error if given URL does not point to a valid asset or the URL is read-only.
deleteAsset.return_description: Returns true if asset was deleted successfully, false otherwise

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to the asset that should be removed.

deleteFolder.title: MachineLibrary.deleteFolder Method
deleteFolder.description: Delete folder by URL from the library. Any content of the folder will also be deleted. Throw an error if given URL does not point to a valid folder or the URL is read-only.
deleteFolder.return_description: Returns true if folder was deleted successfully, false otherwise

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to the folder that should be removed.

displayName.title: MachineLibrary.displayName Method
displayName.description: Get the localized display name for a given URL. The URL must point to a folder.
displayName.return_description: Returns localized display name for the folder. Returns empty string for invalid URL.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL that defines the path to a folder.

doesPathExist.title: MachineLibrary.doesPathExist Method
doesPathExist.description: Checks if the given URL points to an existing folder or asset in the library.
doesPathExist.return_description: Returns true if the URL points to an existing folder or asset, false otherwise.

importMachine.title: MachineLibrary.importMachine Method
importMachine.description: Import a given machine at a specific location. The machine will be stored in the library. Throws an error, if the given URL is read-only.
importMachine.return_description: Returns the URL of the newly imported machine, or null if the import failed.

parameters.name: machine
parameters.description: The machine that should be imported.

parameters.name: destinationUrl
parameters.description: The URL to the folder where to save the machine.

parameters.name: machineName
parameters.description: The name of the machine that should be created due to the import. The name can be extended if the asset already exists at given location to ensure a unique name.

machineAtURL.title: MachineLibrary.machineAtURL Method
machineAtURL.description: Get a specific machine by the given URL. Returns null, if the URL does not exist.
machineAtURL.return_description: Returns the machine for a valid URL, returns null otherwise.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to the machine to be loaded.

updateMachine.title: MachineLibrary.updateMachine Method
updateMachine.description: Update a machine in the library. The library overrides the URL by given machine. Throws an error if the URL does not already point to an existing machine.
updateMachine.return_description: Returns true if asset was updated successfully, false otherwise.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to the existing asset in the library that should be updated.

parameters.name: machine
parameters.description: The machine that should be persisted.

urlByLocation.title: MachineLibrary.urlByLocation Method
urlByLocation.description: Get the URL for a given LibraryLocations.
urlByLocation.return_description: Returns the URL for given location.

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: The LibraryLocations to be converted into an URL.
